key_insight: Most mortality-driving aging dynamics appear latent rather than captured by current blood biomarkers, and only some known causal proteins show support in the causal-inference tests.
clinical_relevance: This suggests many aging biomarkers may be better for prediction than for identifying true intervention targets, so causal validation is needed before translation.
method: Markov generator-matrix model with death as an absorbing state, fit to NHANES and replicated in HRS.
Two-platform cis-pQTL Mendelian randomization plus colocalization against parental-lifespan GWAS.
Clock comparison in cellular reprogramming using Horvath and DamAge.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports ~92% of Gompertz acceleration attributed to a latent component in NHANES and HRS, with known causal proteins detected but the measured latent proxies largely null.
watch_item: Causal claims depend on the quality of instrument selection, colocalization, and the assumption that the public cohorts and biomarker panels capture the relevant biology.
paper_title: A generator-matrix causal-inference framework separates measurable aging biomarkers from mortality-driving latent dynamics in humans
